examples.title: Algorithm: Validate SMART Objectives
examples.description: Checks if a project objective meets all SMART criteria
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ValidateSMART
BEGIN
 INPUT objective: String
 SPECIFIC = Check if objective defines clear outcome (keywords: "increase", "reduce", "implement")
 MEASURABLE = Check if objective has quantifiable metric (numbers, percentages)
 ACHIEVABLE = Verify resources and timeline are realistic
 RELEVANT = Align with business goals and stakeholder needs
 TIME_BOUND = Check for explicit deadline or timeframe
 RETURN (SPECIFIC AND MEASURABLE AND ACHIEVABLE AND RELEVANT AND TIME_BOUND)
END
examples.explanation:
1. SPECIFIC check verifies clear intent
2. MEASURABLE looks for numerical metrics
3. ACHIEVABLE validates resource feasibility
4. RELEVANT ensures alignment with business goals
5. TIME_BOUND confirms deadline exists

examples.title: Algorithm: Prioritize Objectives with MoSCoW
examples.description: Categorizes objectives into Must/Should/Could/Won't have categories
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MoSCoWPrioritization
BEGIN
 INPUT objectives: List, stakeholders: List
 FOR EACH obj IN objectives
 stakeholder_votes = COLLECT stakeholder input FOR obj.priority
 category = DETERMINE_CATEGORY(stakeholder_votes)
 obj.set_priority(category)
 END FOR
 RETURN prioritized_objectives
END
examples.explanation:
1. Collect stakeholder votes for each objective
2. Determine majority category (Must/Should/Could/Won't)
3. Assign priority based on consensus
4. Return sorted list by priority

examples.title: Algorithm: Objective Dependency Resolver
examples.description: Identifies objective execution order using topological sorting
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DependencyResolver
BEGIN
 INPUT objectives_graph: Graph
 sorted_list = EMPTY_LIST
 queue = objectives_graph.nodes_with_zero_indegree()
 WHILE queue NOT EMPTY
 node = queue.dequeue()
 sorted_list.ADD(node)
 FOR EACH dependent IN node.dependents
 REDUCE_INDEGREE(dependent)
 IF dependent.indegree == 0
 queue.enqueue(dependent)
 END WHILE
 RETURN sorted_list
END
examples.explanation:
1. Initialize queue with independent objectives
2. Process nodes in topological order
3. Update dependencies as objectives are resolved
4. Return optimal execution sequence
